message:
test(fuzz): add ReplayWindow sliding-bitmap state-machine fuzz target (resolves id5bl)

ReplayWindow (session.rs:720-797) is the per-session sliding-bitmap
replay-protection for FCPS frame seq numbers. Distinct from
HelloReplayWindow (covered by p5ams) which is a (from, nonce) FIFO at
the handshake layer. A regression admits replayed seqs (CPU exhaustion;
captured-frame MAC passes twice).

NOT covered by existing fcp-protocol fuzz targets.

Properties:
- seq=0 always rejected
- check is pure (no state mutation)
- check ⇔ check_and_update agreement
- immediate-replay rejected after acceptance
- highest_seq monotonically non-decreasing

Once-gated anchors:
- (a) window_size=128 boundary: highest=200, seq=73 (diff=127) accept,
  seq=72 (diff=128) reject
- (b) Large jump (1→200) clears old bits — seq=1 must reject after
- (c) Immediate replay: seq=10 accepted, second seq=10 rejected

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -590,5 +590,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_replay_window"
+path = "fuzz_targets/replay_window.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
